steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Words in Context (completing a sentence with the most precise word). The decisive clue is the phrase "mutually beneficial" and "symbiotic exchange": the tree gives the fungus carbon, so the fungus must give something back in kind.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "overreacts": means to respond too strongly or excessively. A mutual exchange implies a proportional, equal return — not an excessive one. ✗
  • Choice B — "reciprocates": means to return a benefit in kind. The fungus returns the tree's gift of carbon by enhancing its nitrogen absorption — a direct, equal exchange. ✓
  • Choice C — "retaliates": means to respond to a harmful act with harm. The relationship is explicitly described as beneficial, not hostile. ✗
  • Choice D — "deviates": means to depart from a norm or course. The fungus helping the tree fits the established pattern of the exchange — it does not depart from it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
